message:
fix(parser,render-term): improve diagram detection heuristics and ANSI-aware truncation

Harden content-based diagram type detection by stripping Mermaid
comments (%% lines) before heuristic matching to avoid false positives
from metadata. Add sequence diagram detection for -->> arrows, note
directives, and arrow+colon patterns.

Simplify sankey/flowchart header matching using the existing
matches_keyword_header helper, and fix it to also accept hyphenated
continuations (e.g., "sankey-beta"). Tighten is_non_graph_statement
to require whitespace after keyword to prevent false matches on
identifiers like "styleSheet".

Fix truncate_display in terminal diff rendering to be ANSI escape
sequence aware — escape sequences now pass through without counting
toward display width, preventing mid-sequence truncation that produced
garbled terminal output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-7l9.2","title":"Configure rust-toolchain.toml and CI/CD pipeline","description":"Create rust-toolchain.toml specifying nightly channel. Create .github/workflows/ with: (1) check job: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. (2) wasm-build job: install wasm-pack, build fm-wasm for wasm32-unknown-unknown. (3) coverage job with cargo-llvm-cov. Add .gitignore for target/, pkg/, *.wasm, node_modules/. Add .ubsignore for test/bench directories. This mirrors the proven CI patterns from FrankenTUI/DCG but adapted for FrankenMermaid's crate structure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.808688690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:33:57.759233676Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:33:57.759210883Z","close_reason":"Implemented nightly toolchain + CI workflow jobs (check/wasm-build/coverage), .gitignore entries, and .ubsignore; validated with fmt/check/clippy/test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7l9.3","title":"Update AGENTS.md for FrankenMermaid project","description":"Replace the DCG-specific content in AGENTS.md with FrankenMermaid-specific guidance while preserving universal rules (no file deletion, git branch rules, Rust toolchain, code editing discipline, backwards compat stance, compiler checks, beads workflow, session protocol). Add: project overview (extracting mermaid engine from FrankenTUI into standalone lib+CLI+WASM), crate structure map, architecture diagram, key files table, testing strategy, the source-of-truth reference at /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid*.rs, and notes about the legacy_mermaid_code/ reference directory (mermaid-js, for format reference only, NOT a port target).

- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:19.316238082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:35:09.071153151Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:35:09.071127944Z","close_reason":"Replaced DCG-specific AGENTS content with FrankenMermaid-specific guidance, crate architecture, source-of-truth references, testing strategy, CI details, and preserved universal safety/workflow rules.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-90m7","title":"ER: Implement relationship label rendering and key type highlighting","description":"# ER Diagram Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Relationship labels (the verb phrase between entities) are parsed but not always rendered prominently
- PK/FK/UK key type highlighting in attribute lists: should use bold/color/icon
- Entity attribute comments (quoted strings after attribute) not rendered
- Cardinality symbols at line endpoints need proper crow's foot rendering

## Implementation Plan
1. Ensure relationship labels render centered on the edge path
2. Add visual distinction for key types (PK = key icon or bold, FK = italic, UK = underline)
3. Render attribute comments in lighter/smaller text
4. Verify crow's foot cardinality symbols are correct for all 14 operator variants","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.640543165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30.983150421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["er","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-90m7","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:42Z"},{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-90m7","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Two entities with ||--o{ relationship and label → verify label renders centered on edge, crow's foot (o{) renders on target end
2. Entity with PK/FK/UK attributes → verify PK renders with key icon or bold, FK italic, UK underline
3. Entity with 'string email UK "Employee email"' → verify comment text renders below attribute
4. All 14 cardinality operators → verify correct endpoint symbols for each
5. Self-referencing entity (EMPLOYEE ||--o{ EMPLOYEE : manages) → verify self-loop with label
6. Entity with no attributes → verify empty entity box renders correctly","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-9cy0","title":"Implement class generics, stereotypes, and annotations","description":"# Class Generics, Stereotypes, and Annotations

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
classDiagram
  class Shape {
    <<interface>>
    +draw()
  }
  class List~T~ {
    +add(T item)
  }
  class Color {
    <<enumeration>>
    RED
    GREEN
    BLUE
  }
  note for Shape "Base interface for all shapes"
\`\`\`
- \`<<interface>>\`, \`<<abstract>>\`, \`<<enumeration>>\`, \`<<service>>\` stereotypes
- \`~T~\` generic type parameters on class names
- \`note for ClassName "text"\` annotations
- \`note "general note"\`

## Current State
- Stereotypes: NOT parsed (<<...>> lines within class blocks are ignored)
- Generics: NOT parsed (tildes treated as part of class name)
- Notes: NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Detect <<stereotype>> lines at top of class block. Parse Class~T~ as class name with generic params. Parse \`note for\` and \`note\` statements.
2. **IR**: Add stereotype: Option<String> and generics: Vec<String> to IrNode for class diagram nodes. Add notes to IR.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Show stereotype in guillemets above class name in header compartment. Show generic params after class name. Notes as yellow sticky-note boxes with connection lines.
4. **Tests**: Interface stereotype, generic class, enumeration, notes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.823305009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:35:22.374838076Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9cy0","depends_on_id":"bd-y2qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:30.577275181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":150,"issue_id":"bd-9cy0","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-aknv","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram activation/deactivation","description":"# Sequence Activation/Deactivation

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>+Bob: Request
  Bob-->>-Alice: Response
  activate Bob
  Bob->>Carol: Forward
  deactivate Bob
\`\`\`
- \`+\` after arrow activates target participant
- \`-\` after arrow deactivates target participant
- \`activate Name\` / \`deactivate Name\` as standalone statements
- Multiple nested activations create stacked boxes
- Activation boxes are narrow rectangles on the lifeline showing active periods

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The +/- modifiers on arrows are ignored.
- No SequenceStatement variant for activate/deactivate.
- Layout does not compute activation box regions.
- SVG renderer does not draw activation boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::Activate(String) and Deactivate(String). Parse \`activate Name\` and \`deactivate Name\` lines. Also parse +/- suffix on arrow operators.
2. **IR**: Add activation tracking. Option: store as a list of (participant_index, start_edge_index, end_edge_index) tuples in a new IrSequenceMeta struct on MermaidDiagramIr.
3. **Layout**: Compute activation box bounds: x = participant center ± half_width, y_start = message y where activated, y_end = message y where deactivated. Handle nested activations with x-offset.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw activation boxes as narrow filled rectangles on lifelines.
5. **Tests**: Simple activate/deactivate, nested activations, +/- syntax on arrows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.455081051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:11:18.670511964Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:11:18.669867805Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence activation/deactivation parsing. Supports both standalone 'activate Name'/'deactivate Name' statements and +/- suffix on arrow operators (Alice->>+Bob). Nested activations tracked via per-participant stacks in IrBuilder. Open activations auto-closed at end of diagram. 4 parser tests added. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aknv","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:24.106138908Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: Also add the missing async message arrow type -) (open arrowhead) to SEQUENCE_OPERATORS. mermaid-js supports: ->>, -->>, ->, -->, -x, --x, -) (async). Currently only 6 of 7 are implemented.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:25Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-aknv","author":"ubuntu","text":"NOTE: The +/- activation modifiers appear AFTER the arrow operator, e.g. 'Alice->>+Bob'. The parser must detect these suffixed on the target participant name, not on the operator itself. This means modifying the sequence message AST parser at line 3851 to strip trailing +/- from target before node lookup.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:31:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.994240852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests

- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root
2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root
3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc>
4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present
5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements
6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes:
\`\`\`
classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
classDef default fill:#fff
class nodeA,nodeB important
\`\`\`

Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding.

## Current State in frankenmermaid
- The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes
- The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured
- The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core).
2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc.
3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes.
4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode).

## Test Plan
- Parse classDef with single property
- Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties
- Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes
- Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes
- Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.643658982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:22.387976601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: classDef is supported in mermaid-js for flowchart, class, state, and potentially other diagram types. The parser implementation should handle classDef lines in the generic pre-processing phase (before diagram-type-specific parsing) so it works across all types. The IR's style_defs field is on MermaidDiagramIr which is shared by all diagram types.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
